name: jvm.memory.used
definition: Measure of memory currently used by the JVM across memory pools. Must filter by context.jvm.memory.type to separate heap from non-heap. Primary signal for heap pressure and leak detection. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached or metrics export disabled; verify agent attachment and OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER setting
cardinality_footguns: context.jvm.memory.pool.name values differ by GC (G1: G1 Old Gen vs Parallel: PS Old Gen); do not group cross-GC without awareness. Avoid context.k8s.pod.name (high cardinality).

name: jvm.memory.committed
definition: Measure of memory committed (allocated from OS and available for JVM use) in each pool. Used as denominator for non-heap pressure when jvm.memory.limit is 0 (Metaspace without MaxMetaspaceSize). UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached or collection disabled
cardinality_footguns: Same GC-specific pool name cardinality as jvm.memory.used. Do not group by pod name.

name: jvm.memory.limit
definition: Measure of maximum memory obtainable for each pool. For heap pools: equals -Xmx; for Metaspace without -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize: may be -1 or very large. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached
cardinality_footguns: Metaspace limit may be -1 (unlimited); use committed as denominator for Metaspace pressure instead. GC-specific pool names vary.

name: jvm.memory.used_after_last_gc
definition: Measure of memory used after the most recent GC event on each pool. This is the live set size — the baseline below which the heap cannot shrink. Best metric for memory leak detection. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached or GC not yet run; appears after first GC event
cardinality_footguns: Only available on heap pools that track collection usage. Non-heap pools may not emit this metric. GC-specific pool names vary.

name: jvm.memory.init
definition: Initial memory that was requested from the OS at JVM startup for each pool. Informational; rarely changes after startup. Development stability status in some OTel versions. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached or Development feature disabled

name: jvm.gc.duration
definition: Duration of JVM garbage collection actions recorded as a histogram. Use percentile(p95/p99) for pause latency; use count with per-second for GC rate; use sum with per-second for GC CPU overhead. Filter context.jvm.gc.action to separate minor (end of minor GC) from major (end of major GC) events. CRITICAL: Histogram support in Tsuga must be verified in Stage 2.
absence_means: Histogram metric — verify that Tsuga exposes percentile aggregations for this metric. If not available as histogram, only sum/count may work.
cardinality_footguns: context.jvm.gc.name values differ by GC implementation. jvm.gc.cause is Development status and may not be available. Do not group by pod name.

name: jvm.thread.count
definition: Number of currently executing platform threads. Includes runnable, blocked, waiting, timed_waiting threads. Does NOT count virtual threads (Java 21+ Project Loom). Use context.jvm.thread.state attribute to filter by state. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached; OR service uses only virtual threads (Java 21+) and platform thread count is near-zero by design
cardinality_footguns: context.jvm.thread.state has only 6 values (safe). context.jvm.thread.daemon has 2 values (safe). Do not group by pod name.

name: jvm.class.loaded
definition: Total number of classes that have been loaded since JVM startup. Monotonically increasing counter. Use per-second rate for class load rate. Class loading spikes correlate with deployments and dynamic proxy generation.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached

name: jvm.class.unloaded
definition: Total number of classes unloaded since JVM startup. Monotonically increasing counter. In most production JVMs with G1 and fixed classpath: near-zero. Rising value with OSGi or dynamic proxies is normal; rising value without dynamic loading = potential classloader leak being partially recovered.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached

name: jvm.class.count
definition: Current number of classes loaded in the JVM (loaded minus unloaded). UpDownCounter — treat as gauge. Development stability status — may not be available in all OTel agent versions. Gate on availability.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached OR Development feature not enabled (gate this widget)

name: jvm.cpu.time
definition: CPU time consumed by the JVM process as reported by the OS (monotonically increasing counter). Use per-second rate to get current CPU time per second. More precise than utilization ratio for CPU throttling analysis in k8s cgroups.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached

name: jvm.cpu.count
definition: Number of processors (CPUs/vCPUs) available to the JVM. Stable gauge — typically constant unless container CPU limits change. Informational for interpreting jvm.cpu.recent_utilization (1.0 = all CPUs saturated).
absence_means: OTel agent not attached

name: jvm.cpu.recent_utilization
definition: Recent CPU utilization ratio for the JVM process. Range: 0.0–1.0; 1.0 = all available CPUs at 100%. Combines JIT compilation, GC threads, and application thread CPU. Gauge — use as-is with average aggregation.
absence_means: OTel agent not attached
cardinality_footguns: Safe — single float value per series. Display as percent (multiply by 100 or use percent normalizer). Value 1.0 = saturation, not 100% of 1 CPU.

name: jvm.system.cpu.utilization
definition: Recent CPU utilization for the whole host system (not just JVM). Range: 0.0–1.0. Development stability status — requires runtime-telemetry-java17 module (Java 17+ OTel instrumentation). Gate on availability. Use alongside jvm.cpu.recent_utilization to compute JVM CPU share.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled; OTel agent < 2.0 or Java 17+ module not loaded (gate this widget)

name: jvm.system.cpu.load_1m
definition: System CPU load average over the last 1 minute (Unix load average). Development stability status. Platform-specific: not available on Windows. Useful for distinguishing JVM saturation from host saturation. Number of threads in run queue.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled OR Windows host (not available on Windows)

name: jvm.buffer.memory.used
definition: Memory used by NIO buffer pools (direct and mapped). Off-heap memory — does NOT appear in jvm.memory.used[heap]. Development stability status. Critical for Netty/gRPC/Kafka services. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled; verify jvm.buffer.* availability in Stage 2 (gate this section if missing)
cardinality_footguns: context.jvm.buffer.pool.name has only 2 values (direct and mapped). Safe. Do not use pod name group-by.

name: jvm.buffer.memory.limit
definition: Total capacity of NIO buffer pools. Denominator for direct/mapped buffer utilization formula. Development stability status. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled (gate with jvm.buffer.memory.used)
cardinality_footguns: Same as jvm.buffer.memory.used — 2 pool name values.

name: jvm.buffer.count
definition: Number of buffers in each NIO buffer pool. Development stability status. Useful for detecting buffer count growing while memory is stable (small many-buffer leak pattern). UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled

name: jvm.file_descriptor.count
definition: Number of open file descriptors as reported by the JVM. Includes sockets and file handles. Development stability status. Critical for services with many connections — approaching the OS fd limit causes java.io.IOException: Too many open files. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled; OTel agent version too old (gate this widget)

name: jvm.file_descriptor.limit
definition: Maximum number of open file descriptors the JVM process may have (OS-level limit). Set via ulimit -n. Development stability status. Denominator for File Descriptor Utilization formula. UpDownCounter — treat as gauge.
absence_means: Development feature not enabled (gate with jvm.file_descriptor.count)

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: JVM runtime metrics do not have direct cost dimensions. Cost monitoring for Java services is better tracked at the infrastructure level (host CPU hours and memory allocation) or via application-level metrics (requests processed per unit of compute). Proxy metrics for cost efficiency: jvm.cpu.recent_utilization (CPU waste) and jvm.memory.used vs jvm.memory.limit gap (over-provisioned heap).
